title: rosa dumalis f. subcalva
intro: Rosa dumalis f. subcalva, also known as the alpine rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rosa dumalis f. subcalva include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, and it can also be used topically to aid in the healing of skin conditions and injuries.
